atoms.source_snippet:
[package]
name = "ritual-precompile-addresses"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "Canonical Ritual async precompile addresses (alloy only)"
license.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true

[dependencies]
alloy-primitives.workspace = true
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the `ritual-precompile-addresses` crate manifest, defining an Alloy-only canonical address crate with workspace license/repository metadata and an `alloy-primitives` dependency.

destinations.evidence: Destination manifest lines 1-10 exactly match source-added diff lines 56657-56666.
Destination workspace includes `crates/ritual-precompile-addresses/` and exposes the crate as a workspace dependency.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The manifest is exact-port preserved and remains wired into the workspace for downstream crates that re-export or consume canonical precompile addresses.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing the workspace dependency should break crates that depend on canonical precompile address constants.

summary: The canonical precompile-address manifest is exact-port preserved with the Alloy primitive dependency and workspace wiring.
